body_excerpt: monthly-report-cron.ts has an early exit when pairs.length === 0 that returns only status and queuedPairs, omitting totalPairs, queuedBatches, and failedBatches.
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.test.ts lines 295-301: MonthlyReportCronResult has totalPairs?, queuedBatches?, failedBatches? as optional. The early-return path (line 134) returns only status+queuedPairs; test interface correctly reflects this.
title: CronResult declares three fields as required that are absent on the early-return path

suggested_notion_body: monthly-report-cron.ts does not export handler return types. Test files use hand-written interfaces + bare as casts. Fix: export Awaited<ReturnType<typeof handlerFn>> from each implementation and import in test. Files: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.ts, monthly-report-cron.test.ts
body_excerpt: Every new interface (CronResult, QuotaResetResult, TrialExpiryResult, etc.) is hand-written in the test file and applied via a bare as TypeName assertion on the handler result.
suggested_notion_title: API: Inngest handler return types not exported - test interfaces are hand-written guesses
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.ts exports no return types (grep for export type returns zero). MonthlyReportCronResult / MonthlyReportGenerateResult are hand-written in the test with bare as casts on lines 322 and 613.
title: M1 - Asserted interfaces are not derived from implementation types

body_excerpt: The ESLint rule checks the source line of jest.mock( OR the line of the first string argument. The gc1-allow annotation lands inside the factory body on line 10.
evidence: ShelfRow.test.tsx line 9: the gc1-allow comment is on the string argument line '../../lib/theme'. eslint-rules/no-internal-jest-mock.mjs line 89 checks hasGc1AllowOnLine(node) || hasGc1AllowOnLine(arg) — arg is line 9 which contains gc1-allow. CCR misidentified the placement; it is correct.
title: GC1 lint bypass in ShelfRow.test.tsx - wrong gc1-allow placement

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/package.json: @tanstack/react-query at ^5.90.21 but persist-client at ^5.100.10 (requires ^5.100.10). Two query-core instances in lockfile. PersistQueryClientProvider may fail silently. Fix: bump @tanstack/react-query to ^5.100.10.
body_excerpt: The persist client at 5.100.10 declares peerDependency @tanstack/react-query ^5.100.10, but the project resolves @tanstack/react-query@5.90.21.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: @tanstack/react-query version mismatch with persist-client (5.90 vs 5.100)
evidence: apps/mobile/package.json lines 25-26: @tanstack/react-query still at ^5.90.21 while @tanstack/react-query-persist-client is at ^5.100.10. Version mismatch unresolved.
title: @tanstack/react-query-persist-client peer dependency version mismatch

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/components/common/SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx lines 12-17: spineStyles array defined inside component body, recreated on each render. No dependency on props or state. Hoist to module level.
body_excerpt: The four-element spineStyles array is defined inside the component body and recreated on every render. It never depends on props or state.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: SubjectBookshelfMotif spineStyles recreated on every render - hoist to module level
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/common/SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx lines 12-17: const spineStyles = [...] is still inside the component body, not hoisted to module level.
title: SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx: spineStyles allocated on every render

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/lib/learning-subject-tints.ts:17 maps history subjects to practiceChipBg (soft). In light mode: rgba(255,255,255,0.86) — near-opaque white, invisible on history subject cards. Swap to practiceHistoryBorder (#edbdc7) or a dedicated token.
body_excerpt: In light mode practiceChipBg = rgba(255,255,255,0.86) - near-opaque white. As the soft background for a tinted subject card that produces a near-invisible surface.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: history subject card soft background near-invisible (practiceChipBg = white/0.86 in light mode)
evidence: learning-subject-tints.ts line 17: { solid: colors.practiceHistory, soft: colors.practiceChipBg } unchanged. design-tokens.ts line 113: practiceChipBg: rgba(255,255,255,0.86) in light mode — near-opaque white, visually invisible as history card background.
title: learning-subject-tints.ts: practiceChipBg as the history soft colour is semantically wrong

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/lib/query-persister.ts persists TanStack Query cache (child profiles, session data) to unencrypted AsyncStorage. No comment documents why this is acceptable (OS sandbox boundary, non-credential data). Add inline comment for COPPA/GDPR-K context.
body_excerpt: query-persister.ts persists the full TanStack Query cache to AsyncStorage, which is unencrypted on both platforms. For an app processing educational data for minors (COPPA/GDPR-K territory).
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: query-persister.ts lacks threat-model comment for GDPR-K data in unencrypted AsyncStorage
evidence: apps/mobile/src/lib/query-persister.ts: 10-line file with no security/threat-model comment. Finding requests at minimum an inline comment acknowledging the threat model for COPPA/GDPR-K data.
title: Query cache persistence stores sensitive data in unencrypted AsyncStorage

suggested_notion_body: packages/database/src/client.ts line 97: neonPoolCache.set(databaseUrl, pool) stores full DATABASE_URL (credentials included) as Map key. Any heap snapshot exposes credentials. Fix: key by sha256(databaseUrl).
body_excerpt: neonPoolCache is keyed by the full DATABASE_URL including credentials. Any heap snapshot exposes the credential as a plain string key.
suggested_notion_title: API: neonPoolCache keyed by plaintext DATABASE_URL — credentials in heap snapshots
evidence: packages/database/src/client.ts line 97: neonPoolCache.set(databaseUrl, pool) uses full DATABASE_URL as Map key. No sha256 hashing applied.
title: packages/database/src/client.ts: connection string used as Map key holds credentials verbatim

body_excerpt: Inngest test utilities (inngest-step-runner.ts, inngest-transport-capture.ts): clean, well-typed; shifting from toHaveBeenCalledWith to toContainEqual makes assertions order-independent.
evidence: Positive review summary with no actionable bug. All items mentioned (inngest utilities, query-keys registry, CI nx affected, palette trim, jest shim, ChildCommandCard routing, neonPoolCache) are implemented. No gap to track.
title: Looks Good (informational positive review)

body_excerpt: Lines 26 and 60 of SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx hardcode rgba values. CLAUDE.md requires shared mobile components to use semantic tokens, not hardcoded colours.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/common/SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx: zero rgba matches (grep confirmed). Lines 22 and 56 use NativeWind semantic classes className="bg-surface/72" and className="bg-surface/42" — compliant with semantic token rule.
title: SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx hardcodes rgba(255,255,255,0.72) and rgba(255,255,255,0.42)

suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/post-session-suggestions.test.ts line 75: beforeEach only calls jest.clearAllMocks(), not mockInngestTransport.clear(). sentEvents array not reset between tests — stale events corrupt assertions. Add mockInngestTransport.clear() to beforeEach.
body_excerpt: Every other test file adopting createInngestTransportCapture in this PR calls mockInngestTransport.clear() in beforeEach. This file only calls jest.clearAllMocks().
suggested_notion_title: API: post-session-suggestions.test.ts missing mockInngestTransport.clear() in beforeEach
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/post-session-suggestions.test.ts line 75: beforeEach calls only jest.clearAllMocks() - no mockInngestTransport.clear(). Confirmed: daily-reminder-send.test.ts:88, email-digest-channel.test.ts:354, feedback-delivery-failed.test.ts:102 all call mockInngestTransport.clear() correctly.
title: mockInngestTransport.clear() missing in beforeEach - stale events from prior test

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/lib/surface-ownership.ts checkFile() (lines 450-463) only iterates imp.named. Namespace imports (import * as X) are collected into imp.namespace but never inspected. Forbidden symbols accessed via namespace import bypass the guard. File: apps/mobile/src/lib/surface-ownership.ts:450
body_excerpt: The module JSDoc says namespace imports are all covered but the detection loop only walks imp.named. A surface file doing import * as progress bypasses the guard entirely.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: surface-ownership guard misses namespace imports (import * as X bypasses boundary check)
evidence: apps/mobile/src/lib/surface-ownership.ts lines 450-463: checkFile iterates only imp.named. imp.namespace is collected by collectImports (line 178) but never checked. A namespace import of a forbidden symbol is invisible to the guard.
title: checkFile silently ignores namespace imports (surface-ownership.ts:468)

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/lib/surface-ownership.ts: buildBarrelMap() builds ~200 lines of barrel resolution but checkFile() never uses _barrelMap or _repoRoot (underscore-prefixed, lines 444-445). Barrel re-exports like export { useProgressInventory as useInv } defeat the guard. Wire barrel map or document the limitation. File: apps/mobile/src/lib/surface-ownership.ts:444
body_excerpt: Roughly 200 lines of barrel-resolution code are plumbed into checkFile but both parameters carry an underscore prefix and are never read. A barrel alias defeats the guard.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: surface-ownership.ts barrel resolution infrastructure never wired into violation check
evidence: apps/mobile/src/lib/surface-ownership.ts lines 444-445: checkFile signature has _barrelMap and _repoRoot both unused inside the function. buildBarrelMap() (lines 408-421) builds the structure but it is never consulted in the violation check. Barrel-aliased forbidden symbols bypass the guard.
title: _barrelMap / _repoRoot are built but never consulted (surface-ownership.ts:449)

body_excerpt: ReportsList.tsx generates only two testID patterns. The third pattern progress-report-row-ID existed in the deleted progress/reports/index.tsx but is never emitted.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/progress/ReportsList.tsx lines 99-111: comment explicitly acknowledges the third testID was intentionally not carried forward — grep confirmed no test or e2e flow targets it (noted inline). Deliberate removal with documented rationale.
title: ReportsList.tsx missing progress-report-row-ID testID pattern

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-library-context.ts:77 uses inline ['library', 'retention', profileId]. queryKeys registry has no library domain. Add queryKeys.library.retention(profileId) and update use-library-context.ts. Without this, key-identity tests do not cover this domain.
body_excerpt: useLibraryRetention uses an inline literal; the library domain is missing from query-keys.ts entirely. Future invalidations have no authoritative factory.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: library retention query key is inline literal — missing from queryKeys registry
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-library-context.ts line 77: queryKey: ['library', 'retention', activeProfile?.id] is an inline literal. apps/mobile/src/lib/query-keys.ts has no library domain (grep for library returns zero results).
title: library retention query key is unregistered (use-library-context.ts:77)

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/components/progress/ReportsList.tsx line 69: localeCompare on YYYY-MM vs YYYY-MM-DD. '2026-05-01' > '2026-05' so week-1 sorts above May monthly card (descending). Fix: normalise monthly sortDate to YYYY-MM-01 before sorting.
body_excerpt: reportWeek is YYYY-MM-DD; reportMonth is YYYY-MM. Lexicographic comparison means week-1 rows sort above the May monthly card in descending order.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: ReportsList sort bug — week-1 rows appear above monthly card at month boundary
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/progress/ReportsList.tsx line 69: .sort((a, b) => b.sortDate.localeCompare(a.sortDate)) still uses plain lexicographic sort. Monthly sortDate is YYYY-MM, weekly is YYYY-MM-DD. '2026-05-01' > '2026-05' so week-1 rows incorrectly outrank the May monthly card.
title: Cross-granularity sort produces wrong order at month boundaries (ReportsList.tsx:62)

body_excerpt: The new error condition is const combinedError = !hasAnyData && isError where isError is the monthly reports error only. If weekly reports fail while monthly succeed, combinedError is false.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress/reports/index.tsx line 26: isError = !hasData && (monthlyReports.isError || weeklyReports.isError) — both errors are combined. progress/index.tsx lines 1009-1010 also correctly ORs both error flags.
title: combinedError ignores weeklyError - weekly failure is silently hidden

suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-library-context.ts:130: useMemo(() => {...}, [data?.subjects]). React Query structural sharing stabilises top-level data but not sub-properties. A new array reference is returned on each background refetch, recomputing the Map. Change dependency to [data].
body_excerpt: React Query returns a new array reference for data.subjects on every background refetch even when data is structurally unchanged. The memo recomputes a new Map on each refetch.
suggested_notion_title: Mobile: useSubjectRetentionMap useMemo dep is data?.subjects — causes unnecessary Map recomputes on refetch
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-library-context.ts line 130: useMemo dependency is [data?.subjects]. useQuery stabilises top-level data reference via structural sharing but sub-properties do not carry that guarantee. Should be [data].
title: useMemo dependency data?.subjects instead of data causes unnecessary recomputes

body_excerpt: Key moved from ['evaluate-eligibility', topicId, profileId] to ['retention', 'evaluate-eligibility', topicId, profileId]. In-memory entries under old root unreachable.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/_layout.tsx lines 560-566: queryClient.removeQueries({ queryKey: ['evaluate-eligibility'] }) is present at app boot to purge old-format cache entries. Migration cleanup implemented.
title: Key moved from evaluate-eligibility to retention/evaluate-eligibility — stale warm cache
